items.title: Man jailed for stomping on head of off-duty police officer in Newtown attack
items.summary: Koby Weaver is jailed over his role in what is described as a "brutal" attack which left an off-duty police officer with serious injuries.

items.title: Jury hears Maree Crabtree, on trial for murdering son Jonathan, allegedly claimed she 'wanted to put something in his drink'
items.summary: A Gold Coast woman accused of killing her son allegedly called him a "potato" with "no life" and claimed she "wanted to put something in his drink so he will just not be here", a jury heard.

items.title: Cowboys claim thrilling Queensland derby victory with last-minute try
items.summary: The North Queensland Cowboys have won a thriller at Lang Park, beating the Brisbane Broncos 35-31. Earlier, the Dragons lost their sixth-straight to start the 2026 NRL season.

items.title: Taiwan's opposition leader Cheng Li-wun has met Xi Jinping in China for the first time
items.summary: Taiwan's opposition leader has done something for the first time in a decade, and experts say it could shape relations between Beijing, Taipei and Washington.

items.title: Anthony Albanese and Singapore's prime minister talk trade, fuel in bilateral meeting
items.summary: Australia and Singapore have agreed to make "maximum efforts" to meet each other's fuel and gas needs as the crisis in the Middle East continues, with the prime minister securing an encouraging but not legally binding deal.

items.title: Brazil cricket player Laura Cardoso takes nine wickets in T20 international to set new record
items.summary: Brazilian cricketer Laura Cardoso has set a new record that is going to be tremendously hard to beat.

items.title: Australia's economy is underpinned by housing, which is why housing won't get cheaper, super boss warns
items.summary: AustralianSuper boss says housing prices unlikely to fall much, because major banks can't afford them too

items.title: Man pleads guilty to dealing with millions in cash from illegal tobacco sales
items.summary: Malek Alhmedai's hire car had $4 million stashed inside, with the money linked to illegal tobacco sales.

items.title: Blayze James Noble charged with murder of Christine Joyce Diane Marshall Hunter
items.summary: A Sunshine Coast man has been charged with murdering his girlfriend in the Northern Territory. Ahead of his appearance in court, her sister has spoken of her family's heartbreak.

items.title: At the site of an Israeli strike in Beirut, locals say 'there's no Hezbollah here'
items.summary: In a residential area in Beirut an entire building had been razed to the ground and a nut shop was destroyed. The locals told the ABC "there's no Hezbollah here".

items.title: Afrika Bambaataa, rap icon accused of sexual abuse, dead at 67
items.summary: The Universal Zulu Nation founder was a beloved hip hop icon before sexual abuse and trafficking allegations surfaced in the 2010s.

items.title: Bribbaree grandmother Nerol Doble's family says there will be 'no forgiveness' for son who set her on fire
items.summary: NSW grandmother Nerol Doble was murdered by her son Shannon James Doble in 2024 after an argument over the use of a jerry can. During a sentencing hearing, family members told the court of the hell his actions have left them in.

items.title: Artemis II crew about to make splash landing on Earth after journey around Moon
items.summary: The Orion space capsule will enter the Earth's atmosphere as a burning fireball, travelling at about 40,000 km/h, splashing down off the coast of southern California. Here's a breakdown of how the spacecraft copes with high temperatures, slows down, and what happens as it splashes down.

items.title: What to watch and listen to this weekend
items.summary: An old favourite, new Courtney Barnett and a tasty Korean vegetarian stew.

items.title: Australia vs Great Britain Billie Jean King Cup: Talia Gibson and Kimberly Birrell lose singles
items.summary: Australia's quest to win a place in the Billie Jean King Cup Finals is off to the worst possible start after losses in both opening-day singles matches.

items.title: Cyclone Maila batters Solomon Islands as it moves through PNG towards Far North Queensland
items.summary: Roads and buildings have been destroyed across multiple Pacific islands as slow-moving Tropical Cyclone Maila heads towards Papua New Guinea, with the eye of the storm set to cross over two remote islands early tomorrow.

items.title: Peter Dutton appointed to Queensland Investment Corporation board by LNP government
items.summary: Mr Dutton's has kept a low public profile since he led the Coalition to defeat at the 2025 federal election, and also lost his seat of Dickson.

items.title: Seal sleeping on road causes Mornington Peninsula traffic delays
items.summary: Locals say they are not surprised the seal, described as a regular visitor, decided to stop for a snooze on the roadway.

items.title: Robert Noel Rogers jailed for life over 2021 murder of Raymond Scopelitis on Sunshine Coast
items.summary: Following a trial held in Brisbane, a jury deliberated for almost two days before returning its verdict.

items.title: Why the Iran war fuel crisis will get worse before it gets better
items.summary: The official pledges of the last few days may have placated markets at first, but they have become little more than bargaining chips ahead of this weekend's negotiations between the US and Iran.

items.title: Family of NSW surfer Guy Haymes deliver victim impact statements
items.summary: Guy Haymes was left in a vegetative state after an assault during a dispute over $50 in February, 2024.

items.title: Pilbara residents launch petition after warning system confusion during cyclone
items.summary: Residents in the Pilbara raise concerns over the state's warning system for cyclones following ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle last month.

items.title: Rainy weather easing for AFL Gather Round weekend matches, as Barossa welcomes fans
items.summary: The Bureau of Meteorology says wet weather conditions will improve for AFL Gather Round matches as the weekend progresses, as the premier shares his ongoing bid to keep the event in South Australia.

items.title: Second midwife charged with manslaughter after failed Newcastle homebirth
items.summary: Jordan Michaela Holland is the second midwife charged with manslaughter over the death of a baby after a failed homebirth in Newcastle.

items.title: Penrith Panthers player Casey McLean faces suspension for high tackle in loss to Canterbury
items.summary: Penrith centre Casey McLean is facing a one-week suspension for a high shot that left Canterbury prop Max King with a broken jaw.

items.title: High Court urged to throw out reduced sentence for teen killer of Emma Lovell
items.summary: Lawyers for the Queensland government have travelled to Canberra to urge the High Court to toss out a reduced sentence for a teen jailed for killing Emma Lovell in 2022.

items.title: Chimpanzees in Uganda's Kibale National Park wage lethal 'civil war'
items.summary: A decades-long study reveals hundreds of chimpanzees waging a violent and deadly conflict against their former friends in their remote forest home.

items.title: Chimpanzees made "territorial patrols'
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Chimpanzee's in Uganda's Kibale National Park fight
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Hills Shire Mayor leads calls to simplify governance of Hawkesbury River
items.summary: The Hawkesbury-Nepean River flows through eight NSW council areas and is looked after by 13 agencies, which one mayor says leads to confusion about who is in charge of what.

items.title: Man charged after $1.1 million of diesel fuel allegedly stolen from unmanned Queensland depots over 12 months
items.summary: Police will allege a 48-year-old man used stolen fuel cards to obtain the diesel from unmanned depots over a 12-month period.

items.title: Woman found after week-long search in south-west Victoria
items.summary: The family and friends of a woman who was missing for a week in south-western Victoria have thanked the community after she was found safe and well.

items.title: Sean 'Diddy' Combs's four-year prison sentence questioned in appeals court
items.summary: Sean "Diddy" Combs's lawyers argue his four-year prison term for prostitution-related charges is too harsh.

items.title: Inside the secret Pakistan talks that brokered the fragile Iran war ceasefire
items.summary: For a few volatile hours, the war in the Middle East appeared to be tipping past the point of no return — until a last-minute push of diplomacy pulled it back.

items.title: South West locals say funding, education keys to fixing troubled three-bin waste system
items.summary: With the three-bin FOGO system on shaky ground outside Perth, some locals are urging the WA government to step up and fund a regional processing plant.

items.title: Alex de Minaur sets up Monte Carlo Masters quarterfinal clash with local hero Valentin Vacherot
items.summary: Monegasque star Valentin Vacherot is next on the docket for Australian Alex de Minaur after an impressive win over rising star Alexander Blockx.

items.title: Adventure seekers urged to heed caution after three deaths, many rescues at Sunshine Coast's Glass House Mountains
items.summary: Authorities and experts are urging visitors to Queensland’s popular Glass House Mountains to heed safety warnings after three deaths in as many weeks.

items.title: Cheat Sheet: Charting Euphoria's long and messy road to season three
items.summary: Sam Levinson's controversial teen series has had to overcome four years of actor deaths, character departures and on-set tension to make it to season three.

items.title: Search for missing Murwillumbah woman Yvonne Whear continues in northern NSW
items.summary: The daughter of Yvonne Whear, 73, who went missing in February, says there is no evidence to suggest her mum left her northern NSW home of her own accord.

items.title: Russian President Vladimir Putin declares ceasefire in Ukraine for Orthodox Easter
items.summary: The Russian president declared the ceasefire following Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy proposing one earlier this week.

items.title: ASX 200 and Aussie dollar down, Brent crude and WTI oil up — as it happened
items.summary: The Australian market has finished the trading week on a disappointing note, dropping slightly, despite US stocks rising overnight. Look back on our business blog.

items.title: Low-sensory voting to be introduced statewide at 2026 Victorian election
items.summary: State election voting will be made quieter and calmer for neurodivergent voters in Victoria.

items.title: Southern Queensland farmers take pre-emptive action as dry spell stretches on
items.summary: Queensland farmers gripped by a dry spell are turning to drastic and inventive measures: offloading most of their livestock and laying kilometres of water pipelines.

items.title: Study finds Tasmanian native forest logging increases potential for more severe bushfires
items.summary: Scientists have studied satellite images of a Tasmanian bushfire and found regrowth from extensive logging and recent bushfires has "absolutely" increased the risk of more severe bushfires.

items.title: How the Parkes Dish is helping track Artemis II on its historic journey
items.summary: The Parkes Observatory — also known as The Dish — continues a legacy of supporting lunar missions that started more than 50 years ago, helping Artemis II go where humanity has not since 1972.

items.title: Tiwi Island birder hopes rare photos of endemic owl encourage conservation
items.summary: A traditional owner on a Northern Australian island has captured rare photos of an "enchanting" masked owl, adding to the limited knowledge base surrounding the endemic and culturally significant bird.

items.title: NSW drug diversion scheme disproportionately sends Aboriginal people to court, report says
items.summary: A BOSCAR report finds NSW Police disproportionately used "discretion" to reject eligible low-level drug offenders from a diversion program — especially Aboriginal people.

items.title: Melania Trump criticises Jeffrey Epstein 'lies' and calls for public hearings
items.summary: In an unusual White House address, Melania Trump denies having a friendship with Jeffrey Epstein and complains about "mean-spirited" attempts to smear her.

items.title: Safety review links herb in popular cold and flu supplements with life-threatening anaphylaxis
items.summary: The TGA says people taking popular cold and flu supplements face the risk of life-threatening anaphylaxis from a herbal ingredient.

items.title: Israel's backflip on Lebanon may be posturing, but it's keeping the fragile peace. What you need to know on Iran today
items.summary: The need to ensure the Iran ceasefire holds until the first round of peace talks are held has put Israel under pressure.

items.title: Three Tasmanian MPs share their experiences with mental health and politics
items.summary: A national study has found 70 per cent of politicians feel their job is affecting their mental health. The ABC spoke to three Tasmanian MPs, including the state's premier, about their experiences.

items.title: Adelaide GP Mark Rogers disqualified for professional misconduct amid 'blatant disregard' for previous orders
items.summary: An Adelaide doctor with an "unhealthy obsession" for low-level laser therapy has had his registration cancelled after a tribunal found he demonstrated a "blatant disregard" of previous orders imposed on his registration.

items.title: Sexual misconduct findings permanently added to health practitioners' records
items.summary: More than 100 past and current practitioners have had findings of sexual misconduct added to their public-facing records under new reforms to promote transparency.

items.title: Cyclone Maila weakens to category two as it slowly heads to Queensland
items.summary: Cyclone Maila has been downgraded to a category two system, pushing back the expected time it will make landfall to Tuesday.

items.title: Jog for Joe aims to raise awareness of male eating disorders in Australia
items.summary: Joe Cappello was 25 years old when he died after a 12-year struggle, and the statistics show many more men are at risk.

items.title: Goalkeeper Andrew Redmayne marks 300th A-League game on home turf
items.summary: His career began as a substitute for Mark Bosnich almost 20 years ago, now Andrew Redmayne is joining the A-League's exclusive 300 club.

items.title: Racism erupts after Melbourne announces Little India plans, but some Indians say it will build connection
items.summary: Social media posts about the announcement of a Little India precinct in Melbourne are flooded with racist comments, leaving some Indians with mixed feelings about the plan.

items.title: Suburban neighbourhood developments in south-east Queensland could face delays, report warns
items.summary: South-east Queensland councils are building upwards in a bid to house hundreds of thousands of new residents in the coming years.

items.title: The NDIS's 'social licence' isn't just at risk of eroding – it's close to collapse
items.summary: The NDIS's reputation isn't just at risk of coming under pressure, it's well and truly crumbling, write Nas Campanella and Evan Young.

items.title: TelstraSuper closes Direct Access investment product without properly notifying members
items.summary: A superannuation investment option spruiked as offering "choice and flexibility" has been shut down without proper notification, leaving members less than two months to sell all their assets.

items.title: Construction sector warns of housing delays, mass lay-offs due to Middle East war
items.summary: Contractors, business owners and economists warn that thousands of construction industry businesses could go bust as a result of supply chain disruptions caused by the Iran War, unless the government intervenes.

items.title: Illegal and legal wildlife trade fuelling animal-to-human disease transmission, report finds
items.summary: Of the 2,079 mammal species traded worldwide, 41 per cent share at least one pathogen with humans, according to new data released today.

items.title: How the PM's Singapore visit could help Australia's fuel supply
items.summary: Experts say visits like Anthony Albanese's trip to Singapore are important for securing Australia's energy supplies. As one observer puts it: "When you don't have the stock, you need friends."

items.title: Inside the church of Pete Hegseth, the man who has been leading the US through war
items.summary: The ABC visits US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth's church, whose leaders challenge the legitimacy of democracy and key principles in the US constitution.

items.title: Do you ever need to throw out your old bath towels?
items.summary: Expert tips on getting the most out of your favourite bath towels and when it might be time to replace them.

items.title: Weekly news quiz: A historical orbit, emotional support animals and a tiny outback town
items.summary: It's been a big week in the news. Did you catch these stories?
